description: Create and modify a special apply condition on the zones in the range. This condition gradually “excavates” the material by reducing the stiffness, stresses, and densities of the zones. This keeps spurious inertial effects in the static solution process from causing unrealistic damage to the region around the excavation.

6.0.description: Creates and modifies a special apply condition on the zones in the range. This condition gradually ‘excavates’ the material by reducing the stiffness, stresses, and densities of the zones. This keeps spurious inertial effects in the static solution process from causing unrealistic damage to the region around the excavation.

keywords.name: delete
keywords.description: Finds a previously created zone relax apply condition with name s, and deletes it.

keywords.name: excavate
keywords.description: Creates and initializes an excavation condition on all zones in the range. The following keywords are available to customize how the excavation progresses.

keywords.name: excavate-fish
keywords.description: Specify a FISH function that determines the multiplier to use as the excavation progresses. This should return a value of 1.0 at the start, and 0.0 and the end. If this keyword is used, the default servo is not used.

keywords.name: excavate-fish-local
keywords.description: Specify a FISH function that determines the multiplier to use as the excavation progresses. This function is called separately for each individual zone taking part in the apply condition. If this keyword is used, the default servo is not used.

keywords.name: excavate-name
keywords.description: Assigns the apply condition an identifying name s. If a name is not specified one will be created automatically based on the internally assigned ID number.

keywords.name: excavate-servo-bound
keywords.description: If the current mechanical force ratio (see the zone ratio) is lower than this value, the reduction factor is reduced by the increment. The default value is 1e-3

keywords.name: excavate-servo-increment
keywords.description: If the current mechanical force ratio is less than servo-bound, then the reduction factor is reduced by subtracting f from it. This number should be less than 1.0, and greater than 0.0. The default value is 0.005.

keywords.name: excavate-step
keywords.description: If set to a positive value, then any FISH, table, or servo parameters are ignored and the reduction factor is reduced linearly from 1.0 to 0.0 over the course of i steps.

keywords.name: excavate-table
keywords.description: If specified, then the reduction factor is given by table s. A lookup is performed every step. By default the x value used to lookup a table y value will be the accumulated mechanical time, which for a purely static analysis is the same as the number of steps. The time index used can be changed by using the optional time keyword, with the following options.

keywords.name: excavate-table-step
keywords.description: Use the total cycle count.

keywords.name: excavate-table-mechanical
keywords.description: Use the accumulated mechanical time (the default)

keywords.name: excavate-table-fluid
keywords.description: Use the accumulated fluid flow time.

keywords.name: excavate-table-thermal
keywords.description: Use the accumulated thermal time.

keywords.name: excavate-table-creep
keywords.description: Use the accumulated creep time.

keywords.name: excavate-table-dynamic
keywords.description: Use the accumulated dynamic time.

keywords.name: list
keywords.description: List all relax conditions, including their name and settings.

keywords.name: modify
keywords.description: Finds the previously created zone relax apply condition with name s, and modify its settings. The following keywords are available:

keywords.name: modify-fish
keywords.description: Specify a FISH function that determines the multiplier to use as the excavation progresses. This should return a value of 1.0 at the start, and 0.0 and the end. If this keyword is used, the default servo is not used.

keywords.name: modify-fish-local
keywords.description: Specify a FISH function that determines the multiplier to use as the excavation progresses. This function is called separately for each individual zone taking part in the apply condition. If this keyword is used, the default servo is not used.

keywords.name: modify-name
keywords.description: Assigns the apply condition an identifying name s. If a name is not specified one will be created automatically based on the internally assigned ID number.

keywords.name: modify-servo-bound
keywords.description: If the current mechanical force ratio (see the zone ratio) is lower than this value, the reduction factor is reduced by the increment. The default value is 1e-3

keywords.name: modify-servo-increment
keywords.description: If the current mechanical force ratio is less than servo-bound, then the reduction factor is reduced by subtracting f from it. This number should be less than 1.0, and greater than 0.0. The default value is 0.005.

keywords.name: modify-step
keywords.description: If set to a positive value, then any FISH, table, or servo parameters are ignored and the reduction factor is reduced linearly from the current value (which is at start 0.99) to minimum (by default 0.0) over the course of i steps.

keywords.name: modify-table
keywords.description: If specified, then the reduction factor is given by table s. A lookup is performed every step. By default the x value used to lookup a table y value will be the accumulated mechanical time, which for a purely static analysis is the same as the number of steps. The time index used can be changed by using the optional time keyword, with the following options.

keywords.name: modify-table-step
keywords.description: Use the total cycle count.

keywords.name: modify-table-mechanical
keywords.description: Use the accumulated mechanical time (the default)

keywords.name: modify-table-fluid
keywords.description: Use the accumulated fluid flow time.

keywords.name: modify-table-thermal
keywords.description: Use the accumulated thermal time.

keywords.name: modify-table-creep
keywords.description: Use the accumulated creep time.

keywords.name: modify-table-dynamic
keywords.description: Use the accumulated dynamic time.

7.0.description: Creates and modifies a special apply condition on the zones in the range. This condition gradually ‘excavates’ the material by reducing the stiffness, stresses, and densities of the zones. This keeps spurious inertial effects in the static solution process from causing unrealistic damage to the region around the excavation.

keywords.name: delete
keywords.description: Finds a previously created zone relax apply condition with name s, and deletes it. If the reduction factor has reached 0, the material has been nulled and thus its relax condition has been deleted. For this case, no need to delete it once more.

keywords.name: excavate
keywords.description: Creates and initializes an excavation condition on all zones in the range. The following keywords are available to customize how the excavation progresses.

keywords.name: excavate-fish
keywords.description: Specify a FISH function that determines the multiplier to use as the excavation progresses. This should return a value of 1.0 at the start, and 0.0 and the end. If this keyword is used, the default servo is not used.

keywords.name: excavate-fish-local
keywords.description: Specify a FISH function that determines the multiplier to use as the excavation progresses. This function is called separately for each individual zone taking part in the apply condition. If this keyword is used, the default servo is not used.

keywords.name: excavate-minimum
keywords.description: Note changing the minimum when using the table, fish, or fish-local options to control the reduction factor may result in unexpected behavior, as the time index used for those options will not stop advancing after reaching the minimum value.

keywords.name: excavate-name
keywords.description: Assigns the apply condition an identifying name s. If a name is not specified one will be created automatically based on the internally assigned ID number.

keywords.name: excavate-servo-bound
keywords.description: If the current mechanical force ratio (see the zone ratio) is lower than this value, the reduction factor is reduced by the increment. The default value is 1e-3

keywords.name: excavate-servo-increment
keywords.description: If the current mechanical force ratio is less than servo-bound, then the reduction factor is reduced by subtracting f from it. This number should be less than 1.0, and greater than 0.0. The default value is 0.005.

keywords.name: excavate-step
keywords.description: If set to a positive value, then any FISH, table, or servo parameters are ignored and the reduction factor is reduced linearly from 1.0 to 0.0 over the course of i steps.

keywords.name: excavate-table
keywords.description: If specified, then the reduction factor is given by table s. A lookup is performed every step. By default the x value used to lookup a table y value will be the accumulated mechanical time, which for a purely static analysis is the same as the number of steps. The time index used can be changed by using the optional time keyword, with the following options.

keywords.name: excavate-table-step
keywords.description: Use the total cycle count.

keywords.name: excavate-table-mechanical
keywords.description: Use the accumulated mechanical time (the default)

keywords.name: excavate-table-fluid
keywords.description: Use the accumulated fluid flow time.

keywords.name: excavate-table-thermal
keywords.description: Use the accumulated thermal time.

keywords.name: excavate-table-creep
keywords.description: Use the accumulated creep time.

keywords.name: excavate-table-dynamic
keywords.description: Use the accumulated dynamic time.

keywords.name: list
keywords.description: List all relax conditions, including their name and settings.

keywords.name: modify
keywords.description: Finds the previously created zone relax apply condition with name s, and modify its settings. The following keywords are available:

keywords.name: modify-fish
keywords.description: Specify a FISH function that determines the multiplier to use as the excavation progresses. This should return a value of 1.0 at the start, and 0.0 and the end. If this keyword is used, the default servo is not used.

keywords.name: modify-fish-local
keywords.description: Specify a FISH function that determines the multiplier to use as the excavation progresses. This function is called separately for each individual zone taking part in the apply condition. If this keyword is used, the default servo is not used.

keywords.name: modify-name
keywords.description: Assigns the apply condition an identifying name s. If a name is not specified one will be created automatically based on the internally assigned ID number.

keywords.name: modify-servo-bound
keywords.description: If the current mechanical force ratio (see the zone ratio) is lower than this value, the reduction factor is reduced by the increment. The default value is 1e-3

keywords.name: modify-servo-increment
keywords.description: If the current mechanical force ratio is less than servo-bound, then the reduction factor is reduced by subtracting f from it. This number should be less than 1.0, and greater than 0.0. The default value is 0.005.

keywords.name: modify-step
keywords.description: If set to a positive value, then any FISH, table, or servo parameters are ignored and the reduction factor is reduced linearly from the current value (which is at start 0.99) to minimum (by default 0.0) over the course of i steps.

keywords.name: modify-table
keywords.description: If specified, then the reduction factor is given by table s. A lookup is performed every step. By default the x value used to lookup a table y value will be the accumulated mechanical time, which for a purely static analysis is the same as the number of steps. The time index used can be changed by using the optional time keyword, with the following options.

keywords.name: modify-table-step
keywords.description: Use the total cycle count.

keywords.name: modify-table-mechanical
keywords.description: Use the accumulated mechanical time (the default)

keywords.name: modify-table-fluid
keywords.description: Use the accumulated fluid flow time.

keywords.name: modify-table-thermal
keywords.description: Use the accumulated thermal time.

keywords.name: modify-table-creep
keywords.description: Use the accumulated creep time.

keywords.name: modify-table-dynamic
keywords.description: Use the accumulated dynamic time.

keywords.name: delete
keywords.description: Delete the named relax condition (use the name assigned via the name sub-keyword of excavate/modify), or all conditions if no name is supplied.

keywords.name: list
keywords.description: List all currently-active relax conditions, with their names, scope (zones in range), table (if any), and current relaxation factor.

keywords.name: excavate
keywords.description: Create a relax condition that progressively reduces (excavates) the boundary tractions on zones in the range. Sub-keywords: fish, fish-local, minimum, name, servo-bound, servo-increment, step, table.

keywords.name: excavate-fish
keywords.description: Use the FISH function named s to compute the per-step relaxation factor.

keywords.name: excavate-fish-local
keywords.description: Same as fish but the function name is interpreted in the local FISH namespace.

keywords.name: excavate-minimum
keywords.description: Lower bound (floor) for the relaxation factor, clamped during cycling.

keywords.name: excavate-name
keywords.description: Assign the relax condition a name s so it can be referenced by zone relax delete / zone relax modify.

keywords.name: excavate-servo-bound
keywords.description: Servo-control threshold; the relaxation factor is held when the controlled quantity exceeds this bound.

keywords.name: excavate-servo-increment
keywords.description: Per-step increment applied to the relaxation factor when the servo is active.

keywords.name: excavate-step
keywords.description: Number of cycles over which to ramp the relaxation factor from 1.0 to its target.

keywords.name: excavate-table
keywords.description: Use the named table to drive the relaxation factor versus time. Optional time keyword selects which process clock advances the table: step, mechanical, fluid, thermal, creep, dynamic.

keywords.name: excavate-table-step
keywords.description: Advance the relaxation table against the mechanical step counter.

keywords.name: excavate-table-mechanical
keywords.description: Advance the relaxation table against mechanical time.

keywords.name: excavate-table-fluid
keywords.description: Advance the relaxation table against fluid-flow time.

keywords.name: excavate-table-thermal
keywords.description: Advance the relaxation table against thermal time.

keywords.name: excavate-table-creep
keywords.description: Advance the relaxation table against creep time.

keywords.name: excavate-table-dynamic
keywords.description: Advance the relaxation table against dynamic time.

keywords.name: modify
keywords.description: Modify an existing relax condition (looked up by name s). Sub-keywords are the same as for excavate: fish, fish-local, name, servo-bound, servo-increment, step, table.

keywords.name: modify-fish
keywords.description: Replace the FISH function used to compute the per-step relaxation factor.

keywords.name: modify-fish-local
keywords.description: Replace the FISH function using the local FISH namespace.

keywords.name: modify-name
keywords.description: Rename the relax condition.

keywords.name: modify-servo-bound
keywords.description: Change the servo bound.

keywords.name: modify-servo-increment
keywords.description: Change the servo increment.

keywords.name: modify-step
keywords.description: Change the ramp length (cycles).

keywords.name: modify-table
keywords.description: Switch to a different driving table (and optionally a different time keyword).

keywords.name: modify-table-step
keywords.description: Advance the modified table against the mechanical step counter.

keywords.name: modify-table-mechanical
keywords.description: Advance the modified table against mechanical time.

keywords.name: modify-table-fluid
keywords.description: Advance the modified table against fluid-flow time.

keywords.name: modify-table-thermal
keywords.description: Advance the modified table against thermal time.

keywords.name: modify-table-creep
keywords.description: Advance the modified table against creep time.

keywords.name: modify-table-dynamic
keywords.description: Advance the modified table against dynamic time.
